item.title: Why America is running out of public companies
item.description: The hostile litigation environment amounts to a de facto tax on participating in public markets.

item.title: AI detectors are hurting honest students. Schools should ban them.
item.description: Chatbot detectors are unreliable and their use threatens honest students.

item.title: Maryland’s a lot like Minnesota. It needs a statewide watchdog.
item.description: A patchwork of small oversight units across agencies is no substitute for a strong centralized office.

item.title: The U.S. needs energy from diverse sources to endure supply shocks
item.description: Energy solutions should come from all sources, not just those favored by one political party.

item.title: What Kevin Warsh misunderstands about the Maestro’s method
item.description: The Fed chair nominee says Alan Greenspan trusted anecdotes over data. That’s not true.

item.title: A giant barrier to being self-employed is falling, state by state
What really happened in Islamabad — and what Trump is trying now
item.description: The president is betting economic pressure can do what bombs couldn’t.

item.title: Big spending on social housing is untenable in this economy
item.description: The D.C. mayoral candidate’s dueling plans: Bold and unrealistic vs. modest and achievable.

item.title: The U.S. needs another president like William Henry Harrison
item.description: Readers on the ninth and 10th presidents, and the assimilation bargain.

item.title: The U.S. military is missing out because of Hegseth’s war on Anthropic
item.description: The new Mythos model can detect critical software flaws, but the Pentagon is barring itself from using it.

item.title: Why the Iran negotiations went nowhere. For now.
item.description: Are talks over or just starting? With Iran, it’s hard to tell. Both sides’ wild rhetoric doesn’t help.

item.title: Why democracy still matters in Venezuela
item.description: The specter of socialist dictatorship spooks investors.

item.title: Screens aren’t destroying young minds. I should know.
item.description: Laws restricting phone use won’t solve root causes of adolescent anxiety.

item.title: Hardly anyone checks this little box on their tax return. Why keep it?
item.description: The presidential campaign fund is broken, gutted and pointless.

item.title: Trump wants a bigger military. Congress should attach one condition.
item.description: How to constrain a president who wages war without Congress: Limit the military’s size.

item.title: Harvard’s grade inflation experiment
item.description: Like monetary inflation, runaway A’s in higher education are a collective-action problem.
